RT @pstuffa: @mrejfox @Jasonforrestftw I studied skepticism in school so I find it easy to argue that most things you can’t measure a direc…
@pstuffa @Jasonforrestftw I hear you on that, but do you really think there is no measurable impact of data visualization during this epidemic? It's simply unknowable?

@Jasonforrestftw Unfortunately the change in movement doesn't help me identify WHY people's movements changed, and it could easily have a lot more to do with local and state government measures and traditional public health messaging than it has to do with dataviz - I want to show provable impact

@Jasonforrestftw What I am looking for: a reported out story of a policymaker’s decision rooted in a great dataviz. Or studies about the behavior of people who have/havent seen viz. But it is frustrating when there is this industry “given” benefit that has not been proved out.

@Jasonforrestftw I personally worked on multiple efforts to track and visualize and map hospitalizations, death rates, hotspots, etc. I am not sure anyone who mattered was looking, or that it changed anyone’s behavior, despite the mass of available visualizations

@Jasonforrestftw There’s an overabundance of speculation, I disagree with the evidence part. I am not asking this to be difficult, I really want to find one provable case that isn’t an abstract “more info means better decisions” – which is absolutely not a given
